text:
NEURORADIOLOGY IMAGING REPORT

PATIENT: Diane Castellano
AGE: 55 years
SEX: Female
EXAM: MRI Brain with and without contrast
INDICATION: Staging evaluation. Known Stage IV melanoma with hepatic, lymph node, and subcutaneous metastases. BRAF V600E-mutant. Evaluate for intracranial metastatic disease prior to initiation of systemic therapy.

---

TECHNIQUE

Multisequence MRI of the brain was performed on a 3 Tesla scanner. Sequences obtained include: sagittal T1-weighted localizer; axial T2-weighted fast spin echo; axial T2-weighted fluid-attenuated inversion recovery (FLAIR); axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) map; axial gradient recalled echo (GRE) susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI); axial T1-weighted pre-contrast; coronal T2-weighted thin-section through the posterior fossa; followed by axial, coronal, and sagittal T1-weighted post-contrast images obtained following intravenous administration of 7.5 mL gadobutrol (Gadavist). No prior MRI brain available for direct comparison.

---

FINDINGS

SUPRATENTORIAL BRAIN PARENCHYMA:
The cerebral cortex and subcortical white matter are intact and unremarkable. There is no cortical or subcortical signal abnormality on T2 or FLAIR sequences. No areas of restricted diffusion are identified to suggest acute or subacute ischemia. No evidence of vasogenic edema. The gray-white matter differentiation is well-preserved throughout.

On SWI, there are no foci of low signal intensity to suggest microhemorrhage, hemorrhagic metastatic deposits, or cavernous malformation. No susceptibility artifact beyond expected normal cortical veins.

Post-contrast images demonstrate no abnormal parenchymal enhancement. No ring-enhancing lesions, no nodular enhancing foci, and no leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement in the supratentorial compartment.

The cerebral sulci are appropriate for the patient's stated age. No sulcal effacement. No midline shift.

WHITE MATTER:
There are scattered punctate T2/FLAIR hyperintensities in the bilateral periventricular and subcortical white matter. These are nonspecific and most consistent with mild chronic microvascular ischemic change, appropriate for patient age. These foci do not enhance. No confluent white matter signal abnormality.

VENTRICLES AND CISTERNS:
The ventricular system is normal in size, configuration, and symmetry. No hydrocephalus. The third and fourth ventricles are midline and unremarkable. The basal cisterns are patent and symmetric. No transependymal flow of CSF.

INFRATENTORIAL STRUCTURES / POSTERIOR FOSSA:
The cerebellum is normal in morphology and signal on all sequences. The cerebellar folia are well-defined. No cerebellar parenchymal signal abnormality. No cerebellar cortical or deep nuclei enhancement post-contrast.

The brainstem including the medulla, pons, and midbrain appears normal in morphology and signal intensity. No intrinsic signal abnormality on T2, FLAIR, or DWI. No enhancement.

The fourth ventricle is normal in size and position.

MENINGES:
The dural sinuses demonstrate normal flow void on T2-weighted sequences. No dural-based enhancing masses. No evidence of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. No pachymeningeal thickening or nodularity.

SELLA AND PARASELLAR REGION:
The pituitary gland is normal in size and configuration. The pituitary stalk is midline. No sellar or suprasellar mass. The cavernous sinuses appear symmetric and unremarkable.

CRANIAL NERVES AND SKULL BASE:
The visualized cranial nerves demonstrate normal enhancement patterns and no thickening. The internal auditory canals are symmetric. The skull base demonstrates no marrow signal abnormality or destructive lesion.

CALVARIUM AND SOFT TISSUES:
The calvarium and outer table are intact. There are no lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No extracranial soft tissue mass is identified. The visualized scalp is unremarkable without discrete nodular lesion.

VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
The major intracranial arteries including the internal carotid arteries, middle cerebral arteries, and basilar artery demonstrate normal signal and caliber on time-of-flight sequences. No flow-related signal loss to suggest significant stenosis or occlusion. Vertebral arteries are symmetric.

---

IMPRESSION

1. No intracranial metastatic disease. No parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or pachymeningeal enhancement to suggest metastatic involvement.

2. No acute or subacute intracranial abnormality. No mass effect, midline shift, or hydrocephalus.

3. Scattered punctate periventricular and subcortical white matter T2/FLAIR hyperintensities, nonspecific, most consistent with mild age-related microvascular ischemic changes.

4. Otherwise unremarkable brain parenchyma, calvarium, and visualized skull base.

Reporting Radiologist: [Attending Neuroradiologist, MD]
Department of Radiology — Neuroradiology Division
